description: FundedNext Futures, based in the United Arab Emirates and established in April 2025, is a futures firm offering a 1 Step program. The firm supports trading via Tradovate, TradingView, and NinjaTrader. Verified by our [Intelligence Hub](/) for prop trading excellence.

seo_content:
FundedNext Futures Technical Infrastructure

FundedNext Futures, based in the United Arab Emirates and established in April 2025, is the futures division of the FundedNext brand. The firm offers a single 1-step evaluation structure across three program tiers: Legacy, Rapid, and Bolt. Account sizes range from $25,000 to $100,000, with all three programs offering differentiated payout structures and requirements. As a futures-specific brand launched in 2025, FundedNext Futures extends the established FundedNext ecosystem into the regulated futures market.

The Legacy 1-step program is available in three sizes: $25,000 at $71.99 (original $79.99), $50,000 at $134.99 (original $149.99), and $100,000 at $224.99 (original $249.99). The Rapid 1-step covers the same three sizes: $25,000 at $98.99 (original $109.99), $50,000 at $179.99 (original $199.99), and $100,000 at $251.99 (original $279.99). The Bolt program is listed at $50,000 for $89.99 (original $99.99). Payments are accepted via Apple Pay, Credit/Debit Card, Crypto, Google Pay, PayPal, and Skrill.

Three platforms are supported: NinjaTrader, TradingView, and Tradovate. All instruments are futures-based; no specific leverage data is published for futures contracts. News trading is permitted across all programs, subject to the CME 2% price limit rule. Copy trading is allowed solely for mirroring the trader's own positions. The inactivity threshold differs by stage: challenges require at least one trade within 7 days, and funded accounts require activity within 30 days.

All three programs offer an 80% profit split. Legacy requires a minimum of 5 benchmark days before a payout can be requested, and the withdrawal amount is capped at 50% until 30 days have elapsed. Rapid has no benchmark day requirement, and the withdrawal cap is lifted after the fifth reward. Bolt has a maximum withdrawal of $1,200 per payout and a total cap of 5 payouts on the account. Minimum payout is $250 for $25,000 and $50,000 accounts, and $500 for $100,000 accounts. Payout methods are Crypto and Riseworks.

FundedNext Futures prohibits account rolling using multiple challenge accounts with reckless intent, multi-order spamming, and coordinated trading exploits including depth-of-market manipulation. Bracket strategies — defined as automated predefined buy and sell orders — are banned. Account flipping and inconsistent contract execution using excessively high margin positions are prohibited. The CME 2% price limit rule applies to all accounts and programs. Micro-scalping and order spamming are not permitted regardless of program type.
